01_initial_request.target_name: ALS protein (Insulin-like growth factor-binding protein acid-labile subunit)
01_initial_request.input_data: IGFALS gene variants (c.1349T>C and c.1363_1365delCTC) detected in a short-stature proband via clinical exome and Sanger sequencing
01_initial_request.user_intent: To predict the pathogenicity of IGFALS gene variants initially classified as Variants of Uncertain Significance (VUS) using AlphaFold structural modeling to improve genetic counselling
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Reclassification of the IGFALS variants from Variants of Uncertain Significance (VUS) to potentially pathogenetic based on predicted structural interference

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] A 7.2-year-old girl with short stature (-2.14 SDS) was referred for genetic investigation. [Gap] The causative genetic variants responsible for the short stature phenotype were unknown. [Decision] To achieve identification of pathogenic variants, chose to use Whole Exome Sequencing to perform comprehensive variant detection on the proband's genomic DNA, expecting to obtain candidate gene variants.

tool.name: Whole Exome Solutions kit by Sophia Genetics

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Two IGFALS variants detected: c.1349T>C (p.Leu450Pro) and c.1363_1365delCTC (p.Leu455del), both in exon 2, heterozygous status

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Two IGFALS variants were identified through whole exome sequencing. [Gap] The variants needed confirmation and parental origin determination to establish compound heterozygous state. [Decision] To achieve variant confirmation and segregation analysis, chose to use Sanger sequencing to perform targeted mutation validation on proband and parents, expecting to obtain confirmed mutation status and inheritance pattern.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Confirmed presence of IGFALS mutations; segregation analysis demonstrated paternal origin of c.1349T>C and maternal origin of c.1363_1365delCTC (in trans position)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Two IGFALS variants were confirmed in compound heterozygous state. [Gap] Need to exclude presence of other co-occurring genetic alterations that could contribute to the phenotype. [Decision] To achieve exclusion of additional genetic alterations, chose to use CGH array to perform copy number variation analysis on the proband, expecting to obtain comprehensive genomic alteration profile.

tool.name: Agilent Human Genome CGH oligonucleotide array

parameters.analysis_software: Agilent Feature Extraction and Genomic Workbench 6.5.018 Lite Edition

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Negative results - no other genetic alterations detected

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Two IGFALS VUS variants were confirmed but their pathogenic impact was unknown according to ACMG classification. [Gap] Need to predict the structural impact of mutations on ALS protein function. [Decision] To achieve structural impact prediction, chose to use AlphaFold to perform 3D protein structure analysis on the ALS protein, expecting to obtain structural model showing mutation locations and potential functional interference.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Mutations occurred on two leucine residues forming the same Leucine repeat motif shaping the horseshoe conformation; mutations located on concave side likely interfering with protein-protein interactions; suggested loss of function effect

references.relevance: Reference for AlphaFold protein structure prediction algorithm

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] AlphaFold structural analysis suggested potential pathogenic effect of the variants. [Gap] Need additional bioinformatics evidence to support amino acid tolerance assessment at mutation positions. [Decision] To achieve amino acid tolerance assessment, chose to use SIFT to perform sequence conservation analysis on the mutated positions, expecting to obtain tolerance scores for the variant amino acids.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Only leucine tolerated at position 450; few residues could be tolerated at position 455

03_success_verification.validation_technique: AlphaFold structural modeling combined with biochemical phenotype correlation

height_SDS.interpretation: Significant short stature consistent with IGFALS deficiency

IGF1_level.interpretation: Low levels (-2.2 SD) consistent with IGFALS loss of function

IGFBP3_level.interpretation: Low levels (-2.6 SD) consistent with IGFALS loss of function

GH_response.interpretation: GH excess detected, consistent with reduced IGF1 negative feedback

variant_classification.interpretation: Reclassification achieved based on AlphaFold structural interference prediction

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The initial objective was achieved. Two IGFALS variants initially classified as VUS were reclassified as potentially pathogenic based on AlphaFold structural modeling showing interference with protein-protein interactions on the concave surface of the horseshoe structure, supported by biochemical phenotype (low IGF1, low IGFBP3, GH excess) consistent with IGFALS loss of function.
